rationale: ADR Fitness verdicts are all correct and grounded. ADR-0037 (workflow,execution) cited, adhere: R1 gives gate.go a zero-byte diff, changing only CLI-gathered facts + CLI-layer rendering, preserving the single decision home; Bead 3's gate field stays decision-inert (ADR-0037 2026-07-09 amendment). ADR-0035 (workflow,execution,core) cited, strengthened: non-bead recovery lines drop false 'mindspec complete <bead>' advice; invalid-gate rejection enumerates 5 keys. ADR-0040 (core,workflow) cited, L2/L3 layering respected. ADR-0030 NOT cited is VERIFIED correct: plan.go:466-486 raises adr-cite-irrelevant (error) on empty domain intersection; ADR-0030 domains (execution/validation/lifecycle/lint) do not intersect spec 113's (workflow,core), so citing R1's rev-parse would fail the plan gate. Carry-forward #2 honored: ADR-0034 is Ceremony Collapse (workflow domain), does not carry the gate enum; the enum's real provenance is spec-112-R1's PanelGateKeys (config.go:93-101); plan re-attributes to spec 112 with the correct provenance-correction rationale. Coverage satisfied (workflow+core both covered by the 3 cites); no new ADR needed. Provenance maps all 5 ACs (AC1->B1, AC2->B2, AC3->B3, AC4->B4, AC-global->all) to named runnable tests + verification steps; no orphan AC, no unmapped step.
findings: VERIFIED: internal/validate/plan.go:466-486 emits adr-cite-irrelevant as an ERROR when len(impactedDomains)>0 and intersectFold(ADR.Domains, impacted)==0. ADR-0030 header domains = execution,validation,lifecycle,lint; spec 113 impacted = workflow,core -> zero overlap. Plan's not-cite decision is correct; adding an ADR-0030 cite for R1's rev-parse would trip the gate.

VERIFIED: ADR-0034 title 'Ceremony Collapse - Single-Bead Lifecycle Epic + Legacy Auto-Migrator', domain workflow; it is not the gate-vocabulary home. config.go:93-101 PanelGateKeys doc comment is the enum's declaration (spec 112 R1). Plan's re-attribution to spec 112 for Bead 3's rejection message is correct and more precise than the existing comment (which lists ADR-0034 only as a Related co-reference). Because ADR-0034's workflow domain DOES intersect, the plan correctly uses a provenance-correction rationale rather than a domain-mismatch one.

VERIFIED: ADR-0037 domains workflow,execution intersect workflow; ADR-0035 domains workflow,execution,core intersect both; ADR-0040 domains core,workflow intersect both. adr-coverage (plan.go checkADRCoverage) satisfied: workflow covered by 0037/0035/0040, core by 0035/0040. No coverage-missing risk from omitting ADR-0030/0034.

Provenance: 5 spec ACs (AC1,AC2,AC3,AC4,AC-global) each mapped 1:1 to a bead with concrete named tests (e.g. TestSanitizeNonBeadDecision, TestLoad_EmptyStringModel, TestPanelCreate_GateInvalidRejectedBeforeWrite) and verification steps. No orphan AC; every bead step (1-5/1-4/1-3) is referenced. AC-global correctly fanned to all beads with Bead 1 owning the zero-diff/unmodified-test fence.
No new architectural principle is introduced (bug-fix + deferred-writer completion), so the plan's 'no new ADR needed' conclusion is correct.
